aliases: Indian flatbread with garlic and cheese

english: Indian flatbread with garlic and cheese

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general naan bread entry (FOODON_03302051); no specific garlic-and-cheese naan variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific naan code; A00LC (Bread and rolls) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for garlic and cheese naan. No suitable parent code identified without risk of fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of garlic and cheese naan weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served at a North Indian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Naan bread, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18960) adapted with cheese and garlic additions estimated from ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base naan values taken from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain naan (FDC ID 175035). Cheese contribution (approximately 15 g processed cheese per 100 g product) estimated from ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017, Table 1, Milk and Milk Products. Garlic contribution is minor and does not materially alter macros. Final per-100g values represent a weighted composite of plain naan base (~85%) and cheese (~15%) consistent with a typical restaurant garlic-cheese naan formulation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; white wheat bread and naan analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for garlic-and-cheese naan. GI of 71 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for plain white-flour naan bread (GI ~71, reference food white bread=100 scale converted to glucose scale ~71). Cheese and garlic additions are minor and do not materially lower the GI of the white-flour base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (71 × 55.8) / 100 ≈ 39.6, rounded to 39.8 using 120 g serving × 46.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g = 55.8 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~576 mg per standard 120 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit garlic and cheese naan to occasional consumption, as the sodium from added cheese and butter contributes meaningfully toward the 2000 mg daily sodium target recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI ~71) and high glycemic load per serving (~39.8) from refined white wheat flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Persons with Type 2 Diabetes should treat garlic and cheese naan as a high-GI refined carbohydrate and restrict portion size to half a piece, pairing it with high-fibre vegetables or legumes to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g per 100 g) and sodium from cheese and butter
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, garlic and cheese naan should be consumed in moderation due to its saturated fat and sodium content; choosing plain naan or a smaller portion reduces saturated fat intake in line with guidelines recommending less than 10% of total energy from saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Garlic and Cheese Naan is a leavened Indian flatbread made from refined white wheat flour (maida), enriched with minced or paste garlic and melted cheese, then baked in a tandoor (clay oven) or on a tawa (griddle). The bread is soft, slightly chewy, and pillowy in texture with a lightly charred exterior, a rich buttery aroma from ghee or butter brushed on after baking, and a savoury flavour from the garlic and melted cheese topping. It is a popular variant of the classic North Indian naan, widely served across Indian restaurants in Singapore and globally. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-to-high calorie bread (~295 kcal per 100 g) with a significant carbohydrate load (~46.5 g per 100 g) from refined flour, moderate protein (~9.8 g) from flour and cheese, and moderate fat (~7.2 g) including saturated fat from cheese and butter. Its glycemic index is high (~71), making it a food of concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g) is relevant for hypertension management. It is vegetarian and halal-suitable. Common meal contexts include as an accompaniment to North Indian curries such as dal makhani, butter chicken, or paneer dishes, or as a standalone snack with chutney.
common_user_queries: How many calories in garlic cheese naan?
Is garlic naan high in carbs?
Is garlic and cheese naan vegetarian?
What is the GI of naan bread?
How much sodium is in cheese naan?
Is cheese naan suitable for heart patients?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for garlic-and-cheese naan exists in a primary approved source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy plain naan data with cheese contribution estimated from ICMR-NIN tables. The composite estimation is methodologically sound but introduces moderate uncertainty. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain white-flour naan.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy naan entry or the ICMR-NIN cheese data used for this composite. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary sources used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables for the cheese component, and the USDA naan entry does not provide a reliable selenium figure for this adapted composite. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Grains and staples' as naan is primarily a grain-based flatbread; cheese is a secondary ingredient. The food_name has been normalised from 'Garlic N Cheese Naan' to 'Garlic and Cheese Naan' as the canonical English form. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish requirements (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this food is served in a Singapore restaurant context. The serving size of 120 g is a standard restaurant portion estimate for one piece of naan.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from 'Dairy and eggs' (input) to 'Grains and staples' (correct classification for a grain-based flatbread).
Normalised food_name from 'Garlic N Cheese Naan' to canonical 'Garlic and Cheese Naan'.
Added all five mandatory Singaporean-context multilingual names: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Built comprehensive aliases list and aliases_by_language with full consistency check performed.
Derived per-100g nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy plain naan adapted with ICMR-NIN cheese data.
Assigned GI 71 (high) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for plain white-flour naan with documented estimation basis.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-absence justification in notes.
Populated full health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
Wrote LLM-ready natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with preparation, texture, cultural context, and clinical relevance.
Set ontology codes to closest_parent with gap notes; no codes invented; SNOMED CT set to none with gap note.
